sections.title: Program Structure
sections.description: This page provides a quick syntax overview. Click chapters in the sidebar for detailed documentation.
sections.code:
!start
    # your code here
!end

sections.title: Type Casting
sections.description: Use :Type(expression) to convert between types:
sections.code:
:int n = :int(3.99);    # 3
:float f = :float(5);    # 5.0
:char c = :char(65);     # 'A'

chapters.label: Types & Variables
chapters.searchKeywords: types variables int float char boolean void array list string declaration assignment default values literals binary hex octal decimal 0b 0x 0o 0d type casting :int :float :array :list strong typing static

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: Fractal is statically typed. Every variable must have an explicit type annotation.

sections.title: Declarations
Type Casting
sections.description: Use :Type(expression) syntax:
sections.code:
:float f = :float(42);    # 42.0
:int n = :int(3.99);    # 3
:char c = :char(65);    # 'A'

sections.title: Indexing

chapters.label: Operators
chapters.searchKeywords: operators arithmetic unary binary plus minus multiply divide modulo bitwise NOT AND OR XOR shift left right logical !not !and !or comparison greater less equal not-equal ~= assignment += -= *= /= precedence

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: Fractal uses ~= for not-equal and ! prefix for logical operators.

sections.title: Logical (boolean)
Precedence
sections.description: Operators evaluated in this order (highest to lowest):

chapters.label: Control Flow
chapters.searchKeywords: functions !func return !return control flow !if !elif !else !for !while !break !continue conditionals loops for loop while recursion factorial

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: Fractal has !func, !if, !for, and !while. All blocks use curly braces.

sections.title: Functions

note.text: !func definitions must appear at the top level, not inside any block.

sections.title: Conditionals

note.text: The opening { must be on the same line as the condition.

sections.title: For Loop

note.text: The loop variable must be :int and must not shadow outer variables.

sections.title: While Loop
Recursion
sections.description: Functions can call themselves:

sections.title: Variable Scope

chapters.label: Structs
chapters.searchKeywords: structs struct user defined types :struct member access :: fields nested struct fixed size Vec2 Vec3 Rect Particle initialise self-referential

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: User-defined types that group related fields. Members accessed with ::.

sections.title: Defining Structs
Nested Structs
Initialisation
sections.code:
:struct<Vec2> origin = { x = 0.0, y = 0.0 };
:struct<Vec2> p = { x = 3.0, y = 4.0 };

sections.title: Member Access
sections.code:
:float x = p::x;
:float y = p::y;

sections.title: Structs in Functions
Self-Referential
sections.description: Structs can reference themselves for linked structures:

note.text: Declaring a struct without an initializer produces a warning - structs have no default value.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: Modules organize code across files. Use !module to define, !import to use.

sections.description: A module wraps related code:

sections.title: List Functions
Math Functions
sections.code:
:int a = abs(-5);           # 5
:float s = sqrt(16.0);        # 4.0
:float p = pow(2.0, 3.0);     # 8.0
:int f = floor(3.9);         # 3
:int c = ceil(3.1);          # 4
